objective: Use visual models to identify and verify equivalent fractions

pretest.question: Look at the fraction strips. 1/2 is shaded on one strip. Which fraction on the other strip shows the same amount shaded?

pretest.question: Two circles are the same size. Circle A has 1/2 shaded. Circle B has 4/8 shaded. Do they show the same amount?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 분수 스트립 — 같은 길이 다른 이름
learn.content: EQUIVALENT FRACTIONS are fractions that name the SAME amount, even though they look different. Imagine two same-size fraction strips. Strip A is split into 2 parts, 1 shaded → 1/2. Strip B is split into 4 parts, 2 shaded → 2/4. The shaded area is exactly the same! So 1/2 = 2/4. They are equivalent fractions. 예: A strip split into 3 parts has 1 part shaded (1/3). Another strip split into 6 parts has 2 parts shaded (2/6). Are they equivalent? → Yes, 1/3 = 2/6. (The shaded area on both strips is the same size. 1/3 and 2/6 name the same amount.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 면적 모델 — 같은 색칠 면적
learn.content: You can also use CIRCLES to see equivalent fractions. Draw two same-size circles. Circle A: split into 2 equal parts, shade 1 → 1/2. Circle B: split into 8 equal parts, shade 4 → 4/8. The shaded areas match. So 1/2 = 4/8. 예: A circle split into 4 parts has 2 parts shaded. Another circle split into 8 parts has 4 parts shaded. Are they equivalent? → Yes, 2/4 = 4/8. (Both circles have exactly half shaded. 2/4 = 4/8.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 수직선 — 같은 점, 다른 분수
learn.content: You can also use a NUMBER LINE. Mark 1/2 on one number line and 3/6 on another. They land on the exact same point! Same point = same value = equivalent fractions. 1/2 = 3/6. 예: On a number line, 2/4 and 1/2 land on the same point. What does this tell you? → 2/4 and 1/2 are equivalent fractions. (Same point on the number line means the same value. 2/4 = 1/2.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 동치 분수 정의 — 같은 양
learn.content: 동치 분수란: 다르게 적었지만 같은 양을 나타내는 분수.   1/2 = 2/4 = 4/8 (같은 양, 다른 표기).   2/3 = 4/6 = 6/9. 🔍 핵심: 그림/수직선/막대에서 같은 자리를 차지함. 다르게 보여도 가치는 같음.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 두 배 패턴 — 분자/분모 같은 수만큼 곱
learn.content: 분수의 분자와 분모에 같은 수를 곱하면 동치 분수.   1/2 → ×2 → 2/4 → ×2 → 4/8 (모두 동치)   2/3 → ×2 → 4/6 → ×2 → 8/12 🔍 직관: 같은 케이크를 더 잘게 나누면 조각 수와 분자도 같이 늘어남. (분자만 곱하거나 분모만 곱하면 다른 분수.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 동치 찾기 3단계 루틴
learn.content: 주어진 분수의 동치 분수 찾기:   단계 1) 모델 그리기 (스트립/원/수직선).   단계 2) 같은 양을 다른 칸 수로 표현.   단계 3) 새 분자/분모 읽기. 예: 1/2 → 2등분 한 쪽 색칠 → 4등분 다시 그려도 같은 면적 → 2/4.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 검산 — 그림 겹쳐 보기
learn.content: 두 분수가 동치인지 검산: 같은 크기 모델에 각각 색칠해 겹쳐 봄.   같은 면적/길이 → 동치.   다른 면적 → 동치 아님. 예: 1/2와 2/4 같은 길이 막대에 색칠 → 둘 다 절반 색칠 → 동치 ✓. 🔍 디지털: 수직선의 같은 점을 가리키면 동치.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 흔한 실수 — 분자만/분모만 변경
learn.content: 흔한 실수 (M03): 분자나 분모 한 쪽만 바꿔서 동치라고 함.   예: 1/2 = 2/2? ❌ (분자만 ×2 — 다른 분수가 됨).   예: 1/2 = 1/4? ❌ (분모만 ×2 — 더 작은 분수). 올바름: 분자와 분모 모두 같은 수만큼 곱. 1/2 = 2/4. 🔍 검산: 곱한 후에도 비율이 같은가?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Look at the two fraction strips. Strip A shows 2/3 shaded. Strip B is split into 6 equal parts. How many parts of Strip B need to be shaded to show the same amount as 2/3?
guided_steps: Step 1: Strip A has 2 out of 3 parts shaded.
Step 2: Strip B has 6 parts. Each third of Strip A lines up with 2 sixths of Strip B.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Use the circle models. Circle A shows 3/4 shaded. Circle B shows 6/8 shaded. Compare: 3/4 ○ 6/8
try.hint: Do both circles have the same amount of shading?
guided_steps: Step 1: Circle A — 3 out of 4 parts shaded.
Step 2: Circle B — 6 out of 8 parts shaded.
Step 3: The shaded area looks the same on both.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Use the number line. 1/3 is marked on the top number line. The bottom number line shows sixths. What fraction on the bottom line is at the same point as 1/3?
try.hint: Find where 1/3 lines up on the sixths number line.
guided_steps: Step 1: Find 1/3 on the top number line.
Step 2: Draw a line straight down to the sixths number line.

practice_r2.question: Emma says 2/8 = 1/4. She drew two same-size fraction strips to prove it. Describe what her strips look like.
practice_r2.answer: Strip A is split into 4 equal parts with 1 shaded (1/4). Strip B is split into 8 equal parts with 2 shaded (2/8). The shaded areas are the same length.

practice_r3.question: Explain how you can use a number line to show that 2/4 = 1/2.
practice_r3.answer: Draw two number lines, same length, from 0 to 1. Top line is split into 2 equal parts — mark 1/2. Bottom line is split into 4 equal parts — mark 2/4. Both marks land at the exact same point, which proves 2/4 = 1/2.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Nate says 2/4 = 2/8 because they both have a numerator of 2. Is he correct? Use a model to explain.
practice_r3.answer: Nate is NOT correct. If you draw two same-size strips, 2/4 covers half the strip but 2/8 only covers a quarter. The shaded areas are different sizes. Same numerator does NOT mean equivalent — the denominators make the pieces different sizes.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.6 'Model Equivalent Fractions' pp.373-376
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Nate says 2/4 = 2/8 because they have the same numerator. Why is he wrong?
choices: A. 동치는 분자/분모 모두 같은 수로 곱/나눔. 2/4의 분모만 ×2 했으니 다른 분수.

feedback.incorrect: 분자/분모 둘 다 같은 수로 변환해야.
